summary: Calibrate CPU energy idle-power constant + skip sub-ms RAPL noise (#71). CPU_IDLE_POWER_FRACTION 0.1 -> 0.7 (active-power semantic), assert_record skips energy band when measured_latency < 1ms. V4 matmul pass_energy 0/60 -> 12/60.

V4-4 Phase A: pytorch_cuda + pytorch_jetson measurers + sweep augmenter + plumbing. Mock-based tests so PR is reviewable on i7 dev box without target hardware. Includes capture-on-target runbooks (bin/v4-capture-{jetson-orin-nano,h100}.sh).

V4-5: KPU consistency-only invariants. Pure-function checks for roofline self-consistency, monotonicity, achieved-below-peak, family monotonicity. avg_power_below_tdp diagnostic xfailed with #81 as tracking issue.

V4-6: dedicated V4 model-validation gate workflow (.github/workflows/v4-validation.yml). Path-filtered, distinct PR check name 'V4 Model Validation', 6 meta-tests pin workflow structure. Plus validation/model_v4/README.md contributor guide.

On-chip BW peaks + L1_BOUND classifier dispatch (#61). Three Optional float fields on HardwareResourceModel; populated i7-12700K, H100-SXM5, KPU-T64 with cited sources; classifier emits Regime.L1_BOUND when populated, falls back to capacity-only path otherwise.

Aspect-ratio-based skinny matmul detection. _SKINNY_ASPECT_RATIO=32 catches max(M,N)/min(M,N)>=32 in addition to existing min<16 check. Per-AR-bucket MAE on Jetson matmul: 32-64 -5%, 64-128 -13%, AR>128 -62%. Closes Category 2 from PR #115.

Per-mapper compute efficiency override. compute_efficiency_overrides_by_op dict on HardwareResourceModel + _get_compute_efficiency_override helper bypasses the AGX-tuned legacy curve when set. Calibrated for Jetson Orin Nano FP16 (matmul 0.70, linear 0.94). Latency-pass +8 matmul, +7-12 linear. Closes Category 3 from PR #115.

Measurement-priority sweep regime augmenter (_augment_from_baseline.py). Rewrites sweep regime labels from baseline measurements when measurement is concrete (ALU/DRAM/LAUNCH); keeps analytical labels for AMBIGUOUS measurements. V4 PASS gain: +20 tier-aware, +15 legacy. Closes the regime-classifier blocker.

3-way hardware comparison + visualizer fixes. compare_hardware.py CLI overlays roofline+latency+energy for i7+Orin Nano+KPU T64 in native precision. --predictions-only mode for HW without baselines. vector_add added to visualizer. Sweep classifier launch-bound bug fixed (compute-only -> max(compute,memory)).

Operator validation expansion plan (5 phases). Activations + SFU modeling, element-wise matrix, softmax + composite-op infra, LayerNorm + RMSNorm, attention head as decomposed graph (no-fusion baseline). 5 GitHub issues filed (#123-#127) for tracking.

Stage 8 KPU silicon floorplanner + 5 GEOMETRY validators. Two complementary views derived from silicon_bin: circuit-class (HP_LOGIC/SRAM/ANALOG/IO library binning, Stage 8a) and architectural-role (COMPUTE/MEMORY/MEMORY_CONTROLLER/IO/CONTROL with true 2D checkerboard, Stage 8b). MCs sized by memory_type + per-channel I/O width (LPDDR ~5:1 stripes, HBM ~1.5:1 microbump arrays); count from sku.kpu_architecture.memory.memory_controllers; off-die DRAM channel labels paired 1:1 with MCs. What-if-all-class-X die estimates surface the silicon cost of mixing tile classes (T768 mixed=1214 mm^2 vs all-INT8=471 mm^2, 61% smaller). cli/show_floorplan.py defaults to architectural view; --view circuit falls back to 8a. Validator count 9 -> 14. WARN-max thresholds during heuristic-v1 keep Phase 6 catalog gate green while findings still surface real signal (T768 has 3.59x compute/memory pitch ratio, 76% whitespace).

embodied-schemas#14: KPU SKU naming convention + 12FDX/N7 sweeps + legacy migration. New self-describing SKU id format kpu_t<count>_<rows>x<cols>_<mem><ch>_<value>nm_<foundry>_<library>; library tag conveys product positioning (fdx/ull energy-eff, hpc datacenter, lpp/pp/ffp perf). 8 new sweep SKUs across GF 12FDX and TSMC N7 HPC at identical operating points so process-node delta is isolatable. 4 legacy stillwater_kpu_t* renamed (preserve all data). GF 12FDX gains sram_access_pj_per_byte / dram_io_pj_per_byte / noc_pj_per_flit_per_hop. Tier vocab expanded entry/mid/high/datacenter -> entry/mid/high/enthusiast/datacenter. Cross-foundry comparison validation: 256-tile / 32x32 PE / 600 MHz @ 0.65V yields 12FDX=22W/214mm^2 vs N7 HPC=10W/59mm^2 at same 315 INT8 TOPS.

graphs#153: derived TDP from V/f/workload + PE-array sweep CLI. New graphs.hardware.kpu_power_model module derives per-thermal-profile TDP from (clock, Vdd, ProcessNode energies, WorkloadAssumption) via 5-term roll-up (PE compute + L2 + L3 + NoC + DRAM PHY + leakage) with V^2*f scaling. apply_pe_array_override(spec, rows, cols) helper rescales PE arrays + ops_per_tile_per_clock for roadmap sweeps; surfaced as cli/generate_kpu_sku.py --pe-array. cli/show_compute_product.py composes SKU + ProcessNode + CoolingSolution(s) into one assembled view with thermal-headroom cross-check panel. Catalog regen for 4 legacy SKUs landed derived TDPs within +/- 0.5W of catalog targets after dropping clocks substantially (t256 default 1400 -> 600 MHz with Vdd 0.75V, matching real-world Orin AGX operating points). Pre-existing test failures from catalog regen also fixed (TOPS pegged to old clocks, xfail no longer xfailing, etc.).

graphs#155: adopt new KPU SKU naming convention; rename legacy refs. Mechanical rename across 30 files: stillwater_kpu_t{64,128,256,768} -> kpu_t<N>_..._<foundry>_<library>. Touched factory modules, mapper base_id strings, loader controller-count map, validator references, 9 test parametrize lists, 3 hardware_registry/ directories with internal spec.json id updates, 6 CLI tool docstring/example/default args, and 2 doc files. cli/list_kpus.py gains dynamic column widths + 3 filter flags (--foundry with short aliases like gf/sec, --node-nm exact int match, --library substring match against last _-segment to avoid false positives like 'lp' matching 'lp5x16' memory tokens). cli/show_compute_product.py gains CSV output per CLI rules. KPURow exports foundry+node_nm fields. Factory function names (kpu_t64_resource_model etc.) and mapper display names (Stillwater-KPU-T64 etc.) preserved as user-facing labels.
